reasoning: Rule A applied: score the data returned, not the tool choice. get_japan_heritage returns 日本遺産 (文化庁 Japan Heritage stories), a completely separate program from UNESCO World Heritage Sites. The response contains zero UNESCO WHS entities — the relevant entity class is entirely absent. The tool_scope_note explicitly flags this mismatch and directs callers to see_also_wikidata_heritage or search_area instead. Groundedness/factual_accuracy score 4 because the 日本遺産 routing metadata is real and authoritative, but irrelevant to the query. Rule B: single root-cause (wrong tool / retrieval failure) drives intent and specificity to 1; other dims drop at most 1.
missing_data: All UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Japan (e.g. Shiretoko, Yakushima, Ogasawara, Hidden Christian Sites, Mozu-Furuichi Kofun, Jomon Prehistoric Sites) — especially lesser-known ones. The correct tool is likely search_area or a Wikidata heritage call scoped to UNESCO WHS (P1435 = Q9259 or similar).
improvement_hint: When query tokens match 'UNESCO', 'world heritage', or '世界遺産', the intent classifier should route to see_also_wikidata_heritage (or a dedicated get_unesco_whs tool) before falling back to get_japan_heritage. The tool_scope_note is helpful but places the routing burden on the calling agent rather than handling it server-side.
